ddls:
CREATE TABLE "stations" (
  "station_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each station */,
  "station_name" TEXT /* Full name of the station */,
  "short_station_name" TEXT /* Short name or alias for the station */,
  "station_number" TEXT /* Unique identifier or number for the station */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the station */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the station */,
  "contact_name" TEXT /* Name of the contact person for the station */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Email address of the contact person for the station */,
  "contact_phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the contact person for the station */,
  "status" TEXT /* Operational status of the station (e.g., active, inactive, maintenance) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Additional details about the station */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "analytes" (
  "analyte_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each analyte */,
  "analyte_name" TEXT /* Full name of the analyte (e.g., Dissolved Bromide) */,
  "analyte_abbr" TEXT /* Abbreviation for the analyte (e.g., DissBromide) */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the analyte (e.g., metals, organic compounds) */,
  "detection_limit" REAL /* Minimum concentration that can be reliably measured */,
  "description" TEXT /* Additional details about the analyte */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analyte_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "samples" (
  "sample_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sample */,
  "station_id" INTEGER /* ID of the station where the sample was collected */,
  "sample_code" TEXT /* Unique code for the sample */,
  "analyte_id" INTEGER /* ID of the analyte being measured */,
  "datetime" TEXT /* Date and time the sample was collected */,
  "result" REAL /* Result of the measurement */,
  "result_sign" TEXT /* Sign associated with the result (e.g., <, =, >) */,
  "units" TEXT /* Units of the measurement (e.g., mg/L) */,
  "method" TEXT /* Method used to collect the sample */,
  "depth" TEXT /* Depth at which the sample was collected */,
  "collector_id" INTEGER /* ID of the person who collected the sample */,
  "qc_status" TEXT /* Quality control status of the sample */,
  "data_status_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data status (public, non-public, etc.) */,
  "temperature" REAL /* Temperature of the water sample at the time of collection */,
  "pH" REAL /* pH level of the water sample */,
  "conductivity" REAL /* Conductivity of the water sample */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sample_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_samples_analyte_id FOREIGN KEY ("analyte_id") REFERENCES analytes ("analyte_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_samples_data_status_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_status_id") REFERENCES data_status ("status_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_samples_station_id FOREIGN KEY ("station_id") REFERENCES stations ("station_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_samples_collector_id FOREIGN KEY ("collector_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notes" (
  "note_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each note */,
  "sample_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sample associated with the note */,
  "note_text" TEXT /* Text of the note */,
  "note_type" TEXT /* Type of the note (e.g., technical, administrative) */,
  "note_date" TEXT /* Date when the note was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("note_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notes_sample_id FOREIGN KEY ("sample_id") REFERENCES samples ("sample_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login date and time */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "sample_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sample being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the sample */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the sample was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access occurred */,
  "device" TEXT /* Type of device used for access (e.g., desktop, mobile) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_sample_id FOREIGN KEY ("sample_id") REFERENCES samples ("sample_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version */,
  "sample_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sample associated with the version */,
  "version_date" TEXT /* Date the version was created */,
  "version_notes" TEXT /* Notes describing the changes made in this version */,
  "version_number" INTEGER /* Version number */,
  "versioned_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_versions_versioned_by FOREIGN KEY ("versioned_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_versions_sample_id FOREIGN KEY ("sample_id") REFERENCES samples ("sample_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_status" (
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each status */,
  "status_name" TEXT /* Name of the status (e.g., Non-Public, Review Status Unknown) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the status */,
  "public_access" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the data is publicly accessible */,
  "review_status" TEXT /* Review status of the data (e.g., pending, approved) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id")
)
